name: Oya The Wind of the Sahel
description:
Oya The Wind of the Sahel

*{Lore Oya is the harmattan --- the dry wind that sweeps the grasslands, carrying red dust from the Ashaani desert. She is neither cruel nor kind. She is the breath that clears the old, the storm that scatters what has grown stagnant, the wind that opens a locked door by rattling it until the latch gives way. Her followers are iconoclasts, slave-liberators, and those who believe that the only way to defeat a corrupt system is to let it collapse under its own weight --- and then scatter the ashes.

In Ashaan, Oya is whispered as the Dust-Rider, a faceless figure who appears during sandstorms to cut the chains of the bound. In the Sahel, she is the Cleansing Wind, invoked before burning a corrupt contract. In Ngomebe, her name is carved on keystones that have cracked --- because Oya taught the stone to break. She does not build. She makes room for those who will.

quote "Oya does not ask permission. She does not wait for the right season. She blows, and the dead leaves fall, and the dust settles on new ground. That is not destruction. That is preparation." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Dust and Wind: the harmattan, sandstorms, the dry breath of the Sahel Change: destroying the old to make way for the new Liberation: breaking chains, opening locked doors, scattering what binds Cleansing: burning corrupt contracts, erasing false ledgers, the red dust that covers old tracks itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to Oya does not bind a creature of settled earth or still water --- they bind a creature of dust and dry wind. Their Thiasos is a small beast or mythological being that embodies the harmattan: a sand-colored gecko that sheds its skin twice as fast as any other, a small horned viper that leaves no tracks in loose sand, a dust-bathed finch that appears only after a windstorm, a scarab beetle that rolls dry dung across the hottest plains, or a pale mouse that nests in abandoned grain silos and vanishes when the wind shifts. This creature requires no food, but it must be allowed to feel the dry wind each day --- a sealed room suffocates it. If neglected for a week, it becomes Compromised (its color fades, and its bearer suffers --1 die on all rolls involving escape or evasion until it next tastes a dust storm).

The Codex of Oya is never a book kept indoors. It may be a collection of cracked clay tablets that must be read in the wind, a scroll of desert leather that crumbles if not exposed to dry air, a bundle of dried reeds tied with red thread, or a single shard of green glass that holds the memory of a storm. Because the harmattan erases what is written, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week in a dry, windy place, allowing the dust to resettle on the pages, or the Rites stored there become faint (treat as Compromised until re-swept).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of Oya may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or garment) and whisper a word of the harmattan. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Stealth or Escape rolls in dry or dusty environments, and the first time each scene the bearer would be restrained (by chains, ropes, or a grapple), they may reroll the escape check (the wind loosens what binds). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to Oya.
summary: Oya The Wind of the Sahel *{Lore Oya is the harmattan --- the dry wind that sweeps the grasslands, carrying red dust from the Ashaani desert. She is neither cruel nor kind.

rites.name: Rite of Oya's Breath
rites.description: (Basic, 4 XP) Scene; Zone; No. Tags: , , Materials: A pinch of red dust blown from the palm, or a dry leaf that has never been wet. Effect: For one scene, you may extinguish any non-magical fire in a zone. The extinguishing is silent and leaves no smoke. Additionally, any open flame within Near range gutters and may be snuffed with a gesture. Push It: The wind carries the extinguishing effect to all fires in Near range. Magical fires are suppressed for one exchange (they flicker but do not go out). Mark 1 SB (Spades) as the unnatural stillness draws attention. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: For one scene, you may extinguish any non-magical fire in a zone. The extinguishing is silent and leaves no smoke. Additionally, any open flame within Near range gutters and may be snuffed with a gesture. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Dust Mask
rites.description: (Advanced, 8 XP) Scene; Self or Ally; No. Tags: , , Materials: A strip of cloth that has been dried in the harmattan for nine days. Effect: You or an ally within Near becomes immune to the effects of sandstorms, dust clouds, and airborne irritants for one scene. Visibility is unaffected for you; you can see through blowing dust as if it were clear air. The cloth wraps itself around your face, cool and dry. Push It: Extend the effect to all allies within Near. The cloth becomes permanently stained red; it may be recognized by those who know Oya's sign. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: You or an ally within Near becomes immune to the effects of sandstorms, dust clouds, and airborne irritants for one scene. Visibility is unaffected for you; you can see through blowing dust as if it were clear air. The cloth wraps itself around your face, cool and dry. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Cleansing Wind
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Extended; Zone; No. Tags: , , , Materials: A corrupt contract, a slave collar, or a false ledger; and a handful of red dust from the Ashaani desert. Effect: Summon a dust storm that lasts one hour within a zone (approximately a city block). All actions within are Desperate due to poor visibility. More importantly, the storm scatters one specific piece of evidence --- a single document, a set of tracks, or a witness's memory of a specific event --- as chosen by the caster. The evidence is carried away by the wind and cannot be recovered by mundane means. The GM is the final arbiter of what constitutes "a single piece of evidence." Push It: The dust storm also reveals one hidden truth --- a secret door, a buried body, a false wall --- as the wind strips away what was hidden. The caster gains the Dust-Marked condition (a red stain on their hands that cannot be washed off for one month; this imposes --1 die on social rolls with those who recognize the mark as Oya's sign). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: 1 hour.
rites.effect: Summon a dust storm that lasts one hour within a zone (approximately a city block). All actions within are Desperate due to poor visibility. More importantly, the storm scatters one specific piece of evidence --- a single document, a set of tracks, or a witness's memory of a specific event --- as chosen by the caster. The evidence is carried away by the wind and cannot be recovered by mundane means. The GM is the final arbiter of what constitutes ``a single piece of evidence.'' \\
